findings.evidence: grep finds no `hadamard`/`Matrix.hadamard` token in the file body; `PosSemidef.hadamard` is defined in mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Matrix/Order.lean:238.
findings.issue: `import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Matrix.Hadamard` is unused — no hadamard/entrywise-product declaration appears in the code (only the docstring). The Schur product theorem the docstring credits to it (`Matrix.PosSemidef.hadamard`) actually lives in `Mathlib.Analysis.Matrix.Order`.

findings.evidence: Every lemma invoked in the proofs resolves to PosDef.lean (lines 144, 297, 418) or Data/Matrix/Mul.lean (vecMulVec, 616); no `≤` on matrices and no `hadamard` is used.
findings.fix: Remove `Mathlib.Analysis.Matrix.Order`; keep only `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Matrix.PosDef`, which transitively provides all used lemmas (posSemidef_iff_dotProduct_mulVec, posSemidef_submatrix_equiv, posSemidef_vecMulVec_star_self, vecMulVec).
findings.issue: `import Mathlib.Analysis.Matrix.Order` is unused — it supplies the matrix `≤` order and the Schur product theorem, neither of which the diff references (no matrix-order comparison, no `mul`/Schur lemma; the `add`/`smul`/`mul` lemmas the description mentions are not in this file).

summary: Placement is correct: generic declarations in the roadmap's suggested PositiveDefinite home. But two of three imports are unused — the entire proof depends only on Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Matrix.PosDef.
